title: arabis hirsuta subsp. hornungiana
intro: Arabis hirsuta subsp. hornungiana is a subspecies of the hairy rock-cress plant, which is a perennial herb native to mountainous regions of Central Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and relieving congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Arabis hirsuta subsp. hornungiana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal benefits, although its use should be supervised by a qualified healthcare professional.
